sentence: Here is the SOAP note based on the provided transcript:
Subjective:
HPI: The patient, Gregory, presents with a nonhealing foot ulcer on the dorsal aspect of the lateral right foot, which has been present for approximately two months.
The ulcer has recently become red and is draining, causing concern for the patient.
The patient reports that he may have gotten the ulcer from walking barefoot on the beach at Dewey Beach.
The patient denies specific pain, but reports occasional throbbing in the foot.
Past medical history: The patient has diabetes, for which he takes medication but does not regularly check his blood sugar levels.
His last A1c is reported to be around 10%.
The patient's PCP has not created a treatment plan for his diabetes.
Review of systems: The patient denies fever, but reports occasional throbbing in the foot.
Current medications: [NOT MENTIONED]
Objective:
Vital signs: Blood pressure, pulse rate, oxygen saturation, and temperature are normal.
Physical exam: The patient has a one by two inch circular wound on the dorsal aspect of the lateral right foot, proximal to the fifth MTP joint.
The wound is red, swollen, and draining, with edema present.
There is fluid coming out of the wound, but no necrosis, odor, or bony exposure is noted.
Test Results: An X-ray of the right foot shows no evidence of osteomyelitis.
Assessment / Problem List:
Assessment: The patient has a diabetic foot ulcer with suspected infection.
Problem list: Diabetic foot ulcer; Suspected infection; Uncontrolled diabetes (status: active).
Plan:
I prescribed clindamycin 400mg twice daily for seven days to treat the suspected infection.
I will perform a debridement to remove dead tissue from the wound.
I will order an ABI (ankle-brachial index) to assess blood supply to the foot.
I will obtain a surgical shoe for the patient to wear while the ulcer heals to reduce pressure on the foot.
The patient will return in two weeks for a follow-up appointment to assess the healing of the ulcer and consider another debridement.
Follow-up: The patient will return in two weeks for a follow-up appointment.
